name: Heavy Clothing / Leather Jacket
description: Heavy clothing or leather jacket. Provides minimal protection against light slashing and bludgeoning; no attribute penalty.

name: Hardened Leather
description: Light armor made of tanned and hardened leather over vital points. Used in medieval and adventure campaigns.

name: Studded Leather
description: Hardened leather reinforced with metal studs or small plates. Enhanced protection against slashing and bludgeoning.

name: Rubber Suit
description: Insulating vulcanized rubber garment. Specific protection against electric shocks; used by technicians facing electrical attacks.

name: Ring Armor
description: Metal rings sewn over leather lining. Intermediate between leather and chainmail.

name: Chainmail
description: Interlocked metal rings covering torso, shoulders and arms. Solid protection against slashing and piercing.

name: Full Chainmail
description: Chainmail covering the entire body including legs and coif. High protection but greatly reduced mobility.

name: Partial Plate Armor (Brigandine)
description: Metal plates covering vital points over leather lining. High protection with good upper-body coverage.
source: TREVAS, 3rd ed. (Daemon Editora, 2004); Medieval Arms Guide — Daemon Editora

name: Full Medieval Plate Armor
description: Steel plate armor covering the entire body. Maximum medieval protection; severe penalty to initiative and mobility.

name: Kevlar Vest (4 layers)
description: Ballistic Kevlar vest with 4 overlapping layers. Basic protection against low-power firearms and knife attacks.

name: Kevlar Vest (8 layers)
description: Reinforced ballistic vest with 8 Kevlar layers. Moderate protection against handguns and bladed weapons.

name: Kevlar Vest (14 layers)
description: Heavy ballistic vest with 14 Kevlar layers. High protection against handguns and submachine guns.

name: Ballistic Vest with Ceramic Plates
description: Kevlar vest reinforced with rigid ceramic or steel plates over vital areas. Protection against rifles.

name: Full Tactical Ballistic Vest (Interceptor)
description: Complete system with Kevlar vest, ballistic plates and additional protectors. Modern military standard (special forces).

name: Flame-Resistant Suit (Nomex/Kevlar)
description: Fire-resistant garment made of Nomex and Kevlar fabric. Used by firefighters and racing drivers.

name: NBC Suit (Nuclear, Biological, Chemical)
description: Hermetic protective suit against chemical, biological and radiological agents. Virtually no kinetic protection.

name: Reinforced Wetsuit / Diving Suit
description: Thick neoprene suit. Light protection against cuts and aquatic hypothermia.

name: Small Shield (Buckler)
description: Round shield worn on the forearm. Grants extra PI on successful Shield test (+2 PI on active block).

name: Large Shield (Pavise)
description: Tall reinforced shield covering most of the body. High extra PI on active block (+5 PI); moderate agility penalty.
